title: Meta-rdk: Raw EEG data
description: This dataset contains raw EEG recordings from a visual discrimination task comparing individuals with schizophrenia spectrum disorders and healthy controls. Participants performed a motion coherence discrimination task with adaptive difficulty while reporting confidence in their perceptual decisions. The study investigates neural correlates of perceptual decision-making and metacognitive processes in schizophrenia.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during a visual discrimination task using 100 moving dots at varying coherence levels adapted via 1up/2down staircase procedure. Participants indicated motion direction (left/right) via mouse click and subsequently reported confidence on a visual analog scale (0-100%). Task difficulty was individually calibrated to match performance between patient and control groups.
